---
title: "Python for Beginners"
number-sections: false
---

::: {.hero}
# Python for Beginners

A friendly beginner course that starts with your first Python program and ends with small useful projects.

[Start the course →](setup.qmd){.btn .btn-primary}
[Go to Lesson 1](lessons/python_lesson_1.qmd){.btn .btn-secondary}
:::

## What this course is

This is a **beginner course**. By the end, you should be able to build small text-based Python programs that ask for input, use variables, lists and dictionaries, make decisions, repeat actions, use functions, save files, handle simple errors, test small functions and create simple charts.

This course deliberately stops before pandas, APIs, object-oriented programming, GUIs and Git. Those belong in a follow-up course.

## Course path

::: {.lesson-grid}

::: {.lesson-card}
### Setup
[Open Setup](setup.qmd)
:::

::: {.lesson-card}
### Positron
[Open Positron intro](lessons/positron_intro.qmd)
:::

::: {.lesson-card}
### Part 1: Getting Started
Lessons 1–3 and Mini Project 1.
:::

::: {.lesson-card}
### Part 2: Loops, Lists and Games
Lessons 4–5 and Mini Project 2.
:::

::: {.lesson-card}
### Part 3: Strings, Functions and Randomness
Lessons 6–8 and Mini Project 3.
:::

::: {.lesson-card}
### Part 4: Files, Dictionaries and Debugging
Lessons 9–11 and Mini Project 4.
:::

::: {.lesson-card}
### Part 5: Becoming a Capable Beginner
Lessons 12–18, Mini Projects 5–6 and the final project.
:::

::: {.lesson-card}
### Project Library
[Open Projects](projects/index.qmd)
:::

:::

## Suggested pace

One lesson per session works well. After every few lessons, try the mini-project before moving on.

::: {.callout-tip}
## Best rule

Do not worry about finishing quickly. The goal is to understand what your code is doing.
:::

## The finish line

You have completed the beginner course when you can independently build a small program that asks for information, stores information, uses functions, saves data, handles simple mistakes and is clear enough that someone else can understand it.
